news.title: Fed RRP Usage Hits Record Low at $503M, Signal of Near-Exhausted Liquidity Buffer
news.desc: Fed data shows ON RRP usage fell to just $503M on April 20, 2026, near record lows. The ON RRP facility, once holding over $2.5T, has been nearly drained, marking a critical transition point for market liquidity. With the RRP buffer essentially gone, bank reserves now bear the full brunt of Fed balance sheet runoff and TGA fluctuations, raising the stakes for future liquidity management.

news.title: Fed H.4.1 Report (Apr 23): Balance Sheet Total at ~$6.7T, TGA at $1,006B
news.desc: The latest Federal Reserve H.4.1 statistical release (April 23, 2026) shows the Fed balance sheet holding near $6.7T. The Treasury General Account (TGA) stands at approximately $1,006B per StreetStats data, a key drain on system liquidity. Reserve balances remain closely watched as the primary buffer against funding stress, with RRP now near zero.

news.title: U.S. Dollar Liquidity Deep Dive: TGA, RRP, and Reserve Dynamics in April 2026
news.desc: A comprehensive breakdown of how TGA balances, ON RRP depletion, and bank reserve levels interact to determine dollar liquidity conditions in April 2026. Explains the shift from RRP-funded liquidity to reserve-dependent dynamics, with practical implications for risk assets. Highlights the formula evolution: Net Liquidity = Fed Balance Sheet - TGA - RRP, now that RRP is nearly exhausted.
